skill.name: when-using-advanced-swarm-use-swarm-advanced
skill.description: Guides advanced Claude Flow swarm coordination with topology selection, agent deployment, performance monitoring, and adaptive optimization.
skill.summary: Coordinate complex multi-agent workflows with adaptive swarm topologies and performance feedback.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command execution patterns. Review confirms these are mostly documented Claude Flow shell examples, not hidden malware, but they still ask users to execute an alpha npm package and run background monitoring commands. Network URL findings are documentation links, and weak cryptography matches appear to be false positives with no cryptographic operation found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Through Alpha NPM Package
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx claude-flow@alpha commands. This is legitimate for the workflow, but npx can download and execute package code, so users should review and pin the package before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command examples are explicit and numerous. Confidence is high that execution risk exists, while malicious intent is not supported by the reviewed context.

medium_findings.title: Long-Running Background Monitoring Commands
medium_findings.description: The workflow includes background monitoring and continuous metric collection examples. If copied without supervision, these commands can consume resources or leave processes running after the user finishes the task.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The background process syntax and loop examples are directly present. The impact depends on user execution, so this remains a medium operational risk rather than a confirmed compromise.

medium_findings.title: Local Workflow Artifacts Written During Execution
medium_findings.description: Several examples write metrics, recommendations, and reports to local files. This is expected behavior, but users should run the workflow in an appropriate project directory and avoid overwriting important files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file output paths are visible in command examples. The risk is limited because the paths are ordinary workflow artifacts and not destructive commands.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Static Match
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to the Claude Flow GitHub documentation. No evidence found that the skill sends data to this URL or performs network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are plain reference links. No fetch, curl, webhook, credential transfer, or automated network call was found nearby.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to be false positives in descriptive text and front matter. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, or credential handling code in the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe advanced swarm coordination and do not perform cryptographic work. The confidence is high because the relevant lines contain no crypto operation.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on npx commands to execute Claude Flow tooling. This pattern is risky when package versions are unpinned or packages are not reviewed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit across quick start, SOP, and full workflow script examples. The risk is inherent to external command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution and Variable Expansion
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow uses shell substitution and variables for timestamps, agent IDs, and scaling values. These examples are normal shell automation, but users should avoid inserting untrusted values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present, but the reviewed examples use expected command outputs and timestamps. Injection risk would depend on untrusted data entering those variables.

dangerous_patterns.title: Background Process Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The process guide starts monitoring commands in the background. This can be useful but should be supervised and cleaned up after metrics collection ends.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The background process markers are directly visible in shell examples. The security impact is operational rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Complex multi-agent work needs clear topology, monitoring, and optimization decisions. This skill provides a structured Claude Flow workflow for adaptive swarm setup and performance tuning.
actual_capabilities: Explains hierarchical, mesh, star, ring, and adaptive swarm topology choices.
Provides Claude Flow commands for swarm initialization and agent spawning.
Shows MCP-style calls for swarm setup, agent metrics, monitoring, and neural training.
Defines validation checklists for each coordination phase.
Guides performance monitoring, bottleneck detection, topology switching, and rebalancing.
Includes a Graphviz process diagram for the full coordination workflow.
limitations: Requires Claude Flow and related MCP tooling to be installed and configured.
Uses shell examples that should be reviewed before execution in sensitive environments.
Does not implement a standalone swarm engine or agent runtime.
Performance improvement targets depend on workload, infrastructure, and agent quality.

use_cases.title: Plan a Multi-Agent Engineering Workflow
use_cases.description: Use the topology guide to choose coordinators, assign specialist agents, and define validation checks before execution.

use_cases.title: Tune Claude Flow Swarm Performance
use_cases.description: Collect baseline metrics, identify bottlenecks, and apply topology or scaling changes with clear success criteria.

use_cases.title: Document Advanced Agent Operations
use_cases.description: Use the phase structure and process diagram to create repeatable operating procedures for complex agent coordination.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Topology
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to recommend a swarm topology for a project with clear task delegation, five agents, and strict review requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Swarm Setup Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a phased Claude Flow setup plan for a coding project with researcher, coder, reviewer, and optimizer agents.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Performance Bottlenecks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze a swarm where latency increased after adding agents. Suggest metrics to collect and likely topology changes.

prompt_templates.title: Design Adaptive Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an adaptive workflow that switches topology, scales agents, rebalances work, and validates performance improvements.

output_examples.input: Plan an advanced swarm for a complex refactoring project.
output_examples.output: A phased coordination plan with topology selection, coordinator roles, specialized agents, validation checks, monitoring metrics, and optimization triggers.

output_examples.input: A swarm has high coordinator load and rising latency.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic path that checks baseline metrics, evaluates mesh or adaptive topology, drains active work before reconfiguration, and compares post-change results.

output_examples.input: Document how the team should operate a Claude Flow swarm.
output_examples.output: A repeatable operating procedure covering initialization, agent deployment, metric collection, dynamic optimization, success criteria, and troubleshooting.

best_practices: Review and pin external CLI package versions before running workflow commands.
Collect baseline metrics before changing topology or scaling agents.
Test topology switches in a non-production workspace before applying them to active work.
anti_patterns: Running every command without checking whether the topology fits the task.
Leaving background monitoring processes active after the measurement window ends.
Treating performance targets as guaranteed results instead of workload-dependent goals.

faq.question: What does this skill help coordinate?
faq.answer: It helps coordinate advanced Claude Flow swarms with multiple agents, topology choices, monitoring, and optimization steps.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documented commands and MCP examples that a user or assistant may choose to run.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The examples focus on Claude Flow workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in production?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review commands, pin external packages, and test topology changes outside production first.

faq.question: What topology should I start with?
faq.answer: Start with hierarchical for clear delegation, mesh for peer collaboration, and adaptive for variable workloads.

faq.question: Does it include performance guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers baseline metrics, bottleneck detection, agent utilization, topology switching, scaling, and comparison reports.
